title: uv — an extremely fast Python package and project manager
text: uv is a single Rust binary from Astral that replaces pip, virtualenv, pyenv and pip-tools. Astral's benchmarks claim 10-100x faster than pip; global cache with hardlinks makes warm installs near-instant.

title: Real Python: uv install benchmark
text: Installing JupyterLab with pip took 21.409s versus 2.618s with uv, roughly 8x faster on a cold cache.

title: Migrated 5 Python projects to uv, cut CI time
text: uv add -r requirements.txt reads requirements.txt and writes pyproject.toml + uv.lock. CI time for a five-project suite fell from 9 min to 4.5 min.

title: Turborepo Remote Caching (docs)
text: Turborepo can share a single cache across the team and CI. The first machine uploads the hashed artifact; every other authenticated machine downloads instead of rebuilding. Enable with turbo login + turbo link or a self-hosted cache.

title: How Makeswift improved CI speed by 65% with Turborepo
text: After adopting Turborepo, Makeswift improved overall CI pipeline time by 65%; commit-to-deploy dropped from ~20 minutes to under one minute.

title: Remote Caching decreased Next.js publish times by 80%
text: Next.js core team used Turborepo Remote Cache to avoid recompiling SWC Rust binaries, dropping publish times by 80%.

title: Prompt caching — Claude Platform Docs
text: Mark prompt blocks with cache_control to reuse a stable prefix. Cache reads cost a fraction of base input; time-to-first-token drops. Minimum ~1,024 tokens; 5-minute default TTL or 1-hour option; pre-warming supported.

title: Anthropic Prompt Caching: how it works
text: Cache reads cost 0.1x base input (90% discount); latency drops more than 2x on cache hits. One developer cut a monthly API bill from $720 to $72.

title: A merge queue is critical infrastructure
text: On April 23, 2026 GitHub's merge queue silently reverted merged code for ~4.5 hours. Branch protection ran on the temporary commit, so the audit trail looked clean. The only signal was that main no longer matched what was merged.

title: The Silent Merge Queue Corruption That Hit 658 GitHub Repos
text: An incomplete feature-flag gate ran a new merge-base computation for squash groups with more than one PR, landing commits that reverted already-merged work. Final impact: 658 repositories and 2,092 pull requests.

title: What happens if a merge queue builds on the wrong commit
text: The commit that lands on main should be identical to what a human would land merging the same PR by hand; verifying that invariant is how you make divergence detectable.

title: Stryker Mutator — what is mutation testing
text: Mutants are automatically inserted into production code and tests are run for each. Killed = a test failed; survived = a gap in assertions. Coverage alone does not measure test effectiveness.

title: Your Tests Are Lying — Mutation Testing in .NET
text: A service with 95%+ coverage still had ~23% of mutants survive. Reviewing survivors surfaced 12 undetected bugs and many missing edge cases.

title: Per-PR Preview Environments When CI Green Isn't Enough
text: CI is structurally blind to five surfaces: deployed-state behavior, real-data shapes, third-party integration drift, browser-side regressions, and cross-component flows. A per-PR full-stack runtime makes them observable before merge.

title: Ephemeral Environments — production-like per pull request
text: Auto-provision an isolated full-stack environment (app + DB + infra) for every PR with a shareable URL; auto-destroy on merge. Database cloning seeds data automatically.
